data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated against the AS-SHIPPED code. VERDICT: clean pairing — NO G4 cross-child finding; findings list EMPTY.

REPO CORROBORATION (current HEAD):
- 8937's surfaces are real and read-side: fetcher.fetch_snapshot exists (fetcher.py:480); the as-shipped contract test (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_snapshot_contract.py:1-29) drives FakeAcliClient → fetcher.fetch_snapshot → inbound differ, asserting schema conformance + a semantic round-trip that READS comment/issuelinks/parent + a scalar exemplar (the bug-0ee6/3f04 seams). _snapshot_schema.py + docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md (the guardrail) exist.
- The schema is INBOUND read-side only: a search for `labels_applied` in _snapshot_schema.py returns NO matches, so 8937 neither asserts nor depends on the outbound telemetry payload that carries the epic's one real cross-child representation conflict (labels list-vs-int, localized to 33d0↔2359 and resolved as-shipped via integer counters).
- File-disjointness: 8937 file_impact = _snapshot_schema.py (new), test_snapshot_contract.py (new), fetcher.py (producer-validates). DISJOINT from every sibling — fe57 (_fakes.py/fixtures/capture script), 59ef (test_verified_fake_contract.py + shared contract, acli_graph.py read-only), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh/test_roundtrip.py/docs). The contract test sits in the shared jira_contract/ dir alongside fe57's _fakes.py, 822a's test_roundtrip.py, and 59ef's test_verified_fake_contract.py as DISTINCT files. 8937 declares depends_on fe57.

7-MODE WALK (parent × 8937):
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 8937's three files are net-new or solely-owned (fetcher.py); no sibling co-edits them. The FakeAcliClient/fixtures are PRODUCED by fe57 and CONSUMED (read, via install()) by 8937 through fetch_snapshot — clean producer/consumer split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 8937's schema pins the inbound read-side shapes (comment nested / issuelinks / parent / scalars); fe57 AC2 (fake serves fixtures through fetch_snapshot with NO shape transformation) feeds exactly those — mutually reinforcing with 59ef (enrichment return-shape fidelity) and 822a (behavioral round-trip of the same shapes). The labels list-vs-int outbound-telemetry conflict is localized to 33d0↔2359 and untouched here (verified: no labels_applied in the schema).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 8937's only input dependency (fe57's fixtures/fake) is declared via depends_on fe57. Fixtures (→fe57), applier collapse (→33d0), telemetry (→2359) are explicitly scoped OUT, consistent with the roster. As shipped, the contract's read-path scope means it does not even require a hard 33d0-before-8937 edge; where it touches "the production path → applier" it binds abstractly (the surviving production path by construction). No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. Four stories drive "the production path" from DISTINCT angles on DISTINCT files: 8937 = assembled snapshot-entry SCHEMA + producer→consumer key/shape round-trip (test_snapshot_contract.py); 59ef = enrichment-METHOD return-shape fidelity (test_verified_fake_contract.py); 33d0 = outbound-only APPLICATION correctness; 822a = BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound behavioral ROUND-TRIP (test_roundtrip.py). Complementary, not duplicative; 8937 declares the others OUT consistently. The documented anti-change-detector guardrail is assigned to 8937 BY NAME in the parent AC ("owned by story B") and in 8937's own description + AC5 (shipped as docs/adr/0004) — an in-scope deliverable of THIS child, not a sibling overlap.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 8937 does not edit the applier files, so it is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57; both are closed in that order.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The new _snapshot_schema.py SSOT that fetcher.py validates against is additive; 8937 AC4 asserts semantic keys/types/round-trip (no whole-blob golden equality, no mock-call-count), consistent with the epic's anti-change-detector posture / parent "lean suite / no net increase in brittle tests." No sibling is forced into a conflicting conformance.
- Residual references — BENIGN. Every schema field references a live producer/consumer surface (fetch_snapshot writes + inbound/outbound differ reads). 33d0 deletes the DEAD typed-leaf applier, but 8937 exercises the PRODUCTION (batch/inbound-read) path, not the typed leaf, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing 8937 references.

NOTE on the documented anti-change-detector guardrail: per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause and 8937's own description ("Owns the epic's guardrail artifact") + AC5 (shipped as docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md), this artifact is OWNED BY THIS CHILD (story B = 8937) — an in-scope deliverable, neither a roster coverage gap nor a G4 cross-child inconsistency for this pairing. (The epic's one genuine cross-child finding — outbound labels list-vs-int — is correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 pairing and resolved as-shipped; it does not implicate 8937, whose read-side schema does not touch labels_applied.)

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 8937): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. Findings list EMPTY.
